title: Question engine core for project intake wizard
description:
Generalize the prompter pattern from `bin/new-task.js` into a reusable question engine that the upcoming `init` wizard (TASK-012) and the existing `new-task` CLI both consume. This is the first ticket in the onboarding-wizard chain (TASK-010..015) that will let a non-technical user clone the framework and have it ask them what they want to build.

The engine must:
- Accept a declarative array of questions with `{id, prompt, type, required, validate, enum, when}` fields.
- Support sequence (ordered) and branching (`when(answers)` predicate gates the next question).
- Re-prompt on validation failure with a human-readable message that names the field.
- Persist partial answers atomically after each question so a Ctrl+C / crash resumes from the next unanswered question on the next invocation — uses `atomicWriteFile` from src/atomic-write.js, writes to a caller-supplied path so the wizard can scope it under `state/sessions/<id>/intake.json` and the new-task CLI can opt out of persistence with `persistTo: null`.
- Be prompter-injectable so tests run with zero TTY (same pattern as `bin/new-task.js`).

As part of the same change, refactor `bin/new-task.js` to delegate to the new engine with its existing question set. This proves the generalization actually works against an existing caller and prevents two divergent prompter implementations from coexisting.

Reference: the design conversation that produced this ticket lives in the 2026-05-26 session — taxonomy (A) curated + escape hatch agreed, this is the first of six tickets (010 engine, 011 question library + PROJECT.md, 012 bin/init.js + routing, 013 agent generator, 014 backlog seeder, 015 UX polish).
acceptance_criteria: `src/question-engine.js` exports `runQuestionnaire({questions, prompter, persistTo, now})` returning `Promise<{answers, completedAt}>` where `answers` is keyed by question `id`.
Question shape supports `{id, prompt, type, required, validate, enum, when}` with `type` in {'string','number','enum','multi'}; `when(answers)` returning false skips that question (branching).

Validation failure (required-but-empty, regex mismatch, enum mismatch, type mismatch) triggers a re-prompt with an error message containing the question's `id` or `prompt` text; the answer is not persisted until valid.

After each accepted answer, the in-progress `{answers, lastAnsweredId}` payload is written via `atomicWriteFile` to `persistTo` (when supplied). A second `runQuestionnaire` call with the same `persistTo` reads the existing file and resumes at the question immediately after `lastAnsweredId`.
`persistTo: null` (or omitted) disables persistence entirely; no temp files appear on disk for that run.

`bin/new-task.js` is refactored to delegate to `runQuestionnaire` for its title/description/AC-count/AC/priority prompt loop; the 4 pre-existing tests in `tests/new-task-cli.spec.js` pass without modification.

New `tests/question-engine.spec.js` covers: (a) sequence with all-string questions, (b) branching via `when`, (c) validation re-prompt path, (d) resume from a half-written persist file, (e) `persistTo: null` writes nothing, (f) atomic-write call ordering (fsync before rename via `vi.mock('node:fs')`).

comments.author: orchestrator
comments.body: Created from the 2026-05-26 design conversation. User wants the framework to be cloneable by a non-technical operator who is then walked through an intake wizard that configures agents and seeds a backlog. We rejected adopting GSD wholesale because of the .planning/ vs tasks/ state-model collision, so we are building our own intake on top of the primitives already in the repo: prompter pattern from bin/new-task.js, atomic writes from src/atomic-write.js, lifecycle bundles for resumability. This ticket is foundational — TASK-011 through TASK-015 all depend on the engine landing first. Workflow: developer writes failing Vitest suite covering all 7 ACs, then implements + refactors bin/new-task.js to delegate, then reviewer in fresh context.

comments.author: orchestrator
comments.body:
Closed. Workflow: tests-first commit `76b2a41` (16 failing specs in tests/question-engine.spec.js, all MODULE_NOT_FOUND for the right reason) + impl commit `6752c8e` (src/question-engine.js new + bin/new-task.js refactored to delegate via 3-phase engine invocation with an `adaptPrompter` shim at the bin/ boundary translating the engine's `{prompt, type, enum, error}` contract into the CLI's existing `(text) => string` contract). Reviewer in fresh context returned **APPROVE-WITH-FOLLOWUPS** (0 HIGH, 1 MEDIUM cosmetic, 7 LOW, 2 NIT). 74/74 vitest specs green in ~6s, atomic-write invariant verified per-rename across two runs (not flaky). All 4 pre-existing new-task-cli specs pass without modification (`git diff f8407d3..HEAD -- tests/new-task-cli.spec.js` is empty).

Design decisions worth recording (the IMPL commit body was empty so capturing here for the trail):

1. **Dynamic-AC handling: option (A) — 3-phase engine invocation, not 2.** Phase 1 prompts title/description/ac_count; phase 2 prompts ac1..acN dynamically; phase 3 prompts priority. The split is necessary because the prompt order (title, description, ac_count, ac1..acN, priority) places the dynamic block BETWEEN ac_count and priority. Two phases would have forced priority before ACs or required `when`-gated synthetic ac_i questions, both worse.

2. **`adaptPrompter` shim at bin/ boundary.** Wraps the legacy `(text) => string` prompter into the engine's `({prompt, type, enum, error}) => string` contract. Error rendering: `(<error>) <prompt> `. This isolation kept the engine API clean and avoided modifying any of the 4 existing CLI tests.

3. **`persistTo: null` from new-task CLI.** The CLI was never resumable and doesn't need to be — the wizard (TASK-012) will pass a real path scoped to `state/sessions/<id>/intake.json`.

AC coverage map: AC1 (signature + completedAt + answers keyed by id) — `sequence_all_string_questions`; AC2 (declarative shape + branching + types) — `branching_skips_when_false`, `branching_includes_when_true`, `multi_accepts_comma_separated_subset`; AC3 (validation re-prompts, not persisted until valid) — `validation_reprompts_on_required_empty`, `..._enum_mismatch`, `..._custom_validate`, `..._number_type_mismatch`; AC4 (per-answer persist + resume from lastAnsweredId) — `persistence_writes_after_each_answer`, `resume_from_partial_state`; AC5 (persistTo:null writes nothing) — `persistTo_null_writes_nothing`; AC6 (CLI delegates without breaking) — diff against tests/new-task-cli.spec.js is empty + all 4 pass; AC7 (sub-coverage of the engine spec including atomic-write ordering) — `atomic_write_invariant` (per-rename fsync-precedes check via vi.mock('node:fs')).

Reviewer findings rolled forward:
- **MEDIUM** (dead-code `validate` on title question in bin/new-task.js): rolled into TASK-015 (UX polish ticket — when that lands it touches the same question array and can drop the redundant `validate` plus rename the `engineerPrompter` typo). Reviewer himself classified as cosmetic, not a correctness defect.
- **LOW** (engine `when()` returning undefined treated as 'ask', silent JSON.parse swallow on corrupt resume file, no re-validation of restored answers): all acceptable at the engine layer — TASK-012 wizard is where strict-resume policy should live.
- **LOW** (empty IMPL commit body): captured here in this close comment so the design decisions are durable.

Smoke test: phase-2 dev ran `node bin/new-task.js --title 'Smoke' --description 'd' --ac one --priority low --label test-only` from repo root, got TASK-011 (key derived from current store correctly), then deleted the file and reverted tasks/index.json via `git checkout --` so `git status` was clean before committing. Ready for the next ticket in the chain — TASK-011 (question library + PROJECT.md schema).
